sections.paragraph:
an unused root.-(I) onomatopoetic, prop. imitating the sound of striking, beating (like the kindred root כָּלַף which see), klappen, klopfen, figuratively applied to the barking of dogs (just as it is said in Germ. der Hund fchlägt an), kläffen, French clapir, clabauder, Swedish glaffa, to bark. Hence כֶּלֶב a dog.

(II) i.q. كَلَبَ to plait, to braid, in the Western languages, with the letters transposed, πλέκω, plico, plecto, flecto, flechten. Hence כָּלַב[ כְּלוּב II. is not given in Thes.].
